B) Do not produce fruiting bodies
C) Do not produce spores
D) Saprophytic protists
62. The slimy mass of protoplasm with nuclei
forms the body of slime moulds is called —
(Pg. 21, E)
A) Plasmodium
B) Myxamoeba
C) Sporocytes
D) Periplasmodium
63. Which of the following is correct about the
slime mould? (Pg. 21, E)
I. Its thalloid body, plasmodium, has
pseudopodia for locomotion and
engulfing organic matter
II During unfavourable conditions
plasmodium differentiates and
produces fruiting bodies, sporangium
III. Spores possess no true cell wall.
IV. They are dispersed by air current.
V. Being extremely resistant, spores
survive for many years
VI. Plasmodium can grow upto several
feet.
A) L,I, 1V, V, VI B) L 11, III
C) 1,11, III, VI D) II, IIT, VI
Paragraph - 2.2.5
Protozoans
64. Protozoans are not included in kingdom
Animalia because — (Pg. 22, E)
A) Mostly asymmetrical
B) Unicellular eukaryotes
C) Heterotrophic nature
D) Multicellular prokaryotes
65. All protozoans are — (Pg. 22, E)
A) Saprophytes only
B) Parasites only
C) Predators only
D) Heterotrophs (parasites or predator)
only
66. Which of the following is considered to be
primitive relatives of animals -? (Pg. 22, E)
A) Dinoflagellates ~~ B) Slime moulds
C) Protozoa D) Protochordata
67. How many major groups protozoan have?
(Pg. 22, E)
A) 3 B) 4
C) 2 D) 8
68. Which of the following are protozoans?
(Pg. 22, E)
A) Diatoms, flagellates, ciliates
B) Desmids, flagellates, ciliates
